old.desc:
```markdown
You must implement a persistent highscore system.
The exact implementation is up to you, but document it in the README.
It must:
- Be stored as you choose (e.g., json file on project, on disk, etc.).
- Be robust to file errors (missing file, invalid format, etc.).
- Handle player names (max 10 characters, alphanumeric and spaces only).
- Handle scores (non-negative integers).
- Keep the top 10 highscores, with player names and scores.
- Load highscores at game start and save them at game end.
- Allow players to enter their name when they end the game (win or lose).
- Display highscores in the main menu.
```

Faulty JSON configuration file

old.desc:
The config file uses JSON. In addition to the standard JSON format, you must handle comments. Lines starting with # are comments and must be ignored. You may also support additional comment styles (e.g., C or C++).

The exact structure is up to you, but document your keys in the README and provide robust defaults.

Suggested keys (names are indicative):

• highscore_filename

• level array of multiple levels

• width , height for each level

• lives : 3

• pacgum : 42

• points\_per\_pacgum : 10

• points\_per\_super_pacgum : 50

• points\_per\_ghost : 200

• seed : 42

• level\_max\_time : 90

On missing or invalid values, clamp to safe defaults, log a clear message, and continue. Unknown keys must be ignored. No traceback.

The game configuration will be updated during the defense.

‌

```json
{
    "highscore_filename": "highscores.json",
    "lives": 3,
    "pacgum": 42,
    "points_per_pacgum": 10,
    "points_per_super_pacgum": 50,
    "points_per_ghost": 200,
    "level_max_time": 300,
    "seed": 42,
    "level": [
        {
            "1": {
                "width": 15,
                "height": 15
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

```shell
```bash
make help

Usage: make <target>
  install - Install dependencies (if using requirements.txt)
  run - Run program (auto-install dependencies with toml config)
  debug - Run with pdb debugger
  build - Build wheel and .tar.gz distributions
  lint - Run flake8 and mypy
  lint-strict - Run flake8 and mypy --strict
  lint-strict2 - Run flake8 and mypy --strict --ignore-missing-imports
  clean - Remove cache files
```

card.desc:
```shell
```bash
make help

Usage: make <target>
  install - Install/sync dependencies from pyproject.toml + uv.lock
  run - Run program without reinstalling dependencies each time
  debug - Run with pdb debugger
  build - Build wheel and .tar.gz distributions
  lint - Run flake8 and mypy
  lint-strict - Run flake8 and mypy --strict
  lint-strict2 - Run flake8 and mypy --strict --ignore-missing-imports
  clean - Remove cache files
```
card.name: Makefile

card.desc:
```markdown
# Compréhension

1. Point d’entrée de paquet\
C’est la déclaration dans `pyproject.toml` sous `project.scripts`.\
Exemple dans ton projet: `pacman = pacman.main:main`.\
Ça signifie: importer le module `main.py,` puis appeler la fonction `main()`.

2. Script local\
C’est un fichier que tu lances directement pendant le dev, par exemple `pac-man.py`.\
Il peut juste servir de passerelle vers le vrai code du package, pratique pour `make run`.

3. Commande installée via uv/pip\
Quand tu fais une installation du package (via `uv sync` ou `pip install`), un exécutable de commande est créé dans l’environnement virtuel à partir de `project.scripts`.\
Dans ton cas, la commande shell `pacman` appellera automatiquement `pacman.main:main`, sans passer par `pac-man.py`.

En résumé pour ton architecture actuelle:

Code applicatif: `main.py`\
Déclaration de commande package: `pyproject.toml:14`\
Optionnel pour le confort dev: `pac-man.py` et `Makefile`
```
card.name: Architecture projet

card.desc:
```markdown
# Créer un package de tout le projet (règle générale)

1. Mettre le code dans un vrai package Python (dossier avec `__init__.py`).
2. Déclarer les packages à inclure dans `pyproject.toml` (`setuptools.packages.find`).
3. Mettre les ressources runtime dans le package (ex: `pacman/assets`) et les déclarer via `package-data`.
4. Charger ces ressources avec un chemin relatif au module du package (pas relatif au cwd).
5. Optionnel mais recommandé: ajouter un script CLI dans `project.scripts` pour lancer l’app installée.
```
card.name: Créer package

old.desc:
Include a Makefile in your activity to automate common tasks. It must contain the following rules (mandatory lint implies the specified flags; it is strongly recommended to try –strict for enhanced checking):

• install: Install activity dependencies using pip, uv, pipx, or any other package manager of your choice.

• run: Execute the main script of your activity (e.g., via Python interpreter). • debug: Run the main script in debug mode using Python’s built-in debugger (e.g., pdb).

• clean: Remove temporary files or caches (e.g., \_\_pycache\_\_, .mypy_cache) to keep the activity environment clean.

• lint: flake8 . and mypy . --warn-return-any --warn-unused-ignores --ignore-missing-imports --disallow-untyped-defs --check-untyped-defs Run with: make lint.

• lint-strict (optional): flake8 . and mypy . --strict Run with: make lint-strict.
